headline: 3 Large-Cap Stocks We Steer Clear Of
summary: Large-cap stocks usually command their industries because they have the scale to drive market trends. The flip side though is that their sheer size can limit growth as expanding further becomes an increasingly challenging task.

headline: Formula 1 and Salesforce Deepen Partnership, Expanding Agentforce to Grow Fan Connection Worldwide
summary: SAN FRANCISCO & LONDON, March 03, 2026--Salesforce (NYSE: CRM), the world’s #1 AI CRM, and Formula 1 (NASDAQ: FWONA, FWONK, LLYVA, LLYVK), the world’s most popular annual sporting series, today announced the launch of a new fan companion agent to scale and personalize engagement for F1’s 827 million global fans.

headline: Analysis-Buyback plans aren't enough to soothe investors after software-sector rout
summary: U.S. software companies have stepped up their stock buyback plans during a months-long rout.  Investors and strategists are skeptical that it will stem the selling.  Investors have been dumping software stocks ‌since the fall, with the S&P 500 software index down 28% since late October, on worries that developments in artificial ‌intelligence (AI) will dramatically disrupt the competitive landscape for the richly valued sector.

headline: Salesforce, Formula 1 Launch AI Fan Agent

headline: Salesforce Recasts Growth Story Around AI Agents And Capital Returns
summary: Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) has launched Agentforce for Communications, extending its AI agent platform into the telecom sector. New collaborations around Agentforce include partnerships with Asymbl and GoDaddy, which is integrating MuleSoft. The company is shifting its model from seat-based software toward digital labor, using AI agents across more customer workflows. Salesforce is also working with Faye and reporting stronger international revenue contributions alongside its growing Agentforce...

headline: A Market Frenzy Is Lurking Beneath Those Calm Stock Indexes
summary: Shares of the memory-chip manufacturer Sandisk have nearly tripled, meanwhile, while Texas Pacific Land, a real-estate company that has become a data-center play, is up 85%.  Cox’s income-oriented, dividend-focused holdings have had a strong year.
